title: Business Management BSc (Hons) Programme Overview & Benefits
text: Business Management BSc (Hons) 2025 Program Overview Award: BSc (Hons) Duration: 3 years Course Type: Full Time Start Date: 08 September 2025 Choosing this programme for 2025 This cutting-edge programme builds deep expertise in a specific management area, while developing skills and knowledge for agility and adaptability to navigate complex business environments. The programme covers foundational management through specialised modules in disciplines of accounting and finance, analytics, marketing, and business management. The programme is taught in English, and content is equivalent to a BSc (Hons) Business Management degree delivered to University of Southampton students in other locations. This programme stands out because:- It provides insights into emergent trends to help students thrive in an ever-changing business environment. The careers and employability focus is suited to a wide range of organisations, including start-ups, large corporations, social enterprises, and the not-for-profit sector. Students will investigate a business topic through a dissertation or a business project in part 3. It is accredited by the Association to Advance Collegiate Schools of Business (AACSB) signalling excellence in business education. What makes the programme unique? Optional modules begin from the second semester itself; students take a high number of optional modules in year 2 to follow their area of interest. The second year envisages that students select six optional modules, or three out of four modules in each semester. Guest lectures and talks are a regular part of the programme for students to network and meet potential employers. In part 3, a year-long dissertation or business project runs along with 6 modules to ensure the rigour of an Honours degree. Which students will benefit the most? Students who are keen to engage with the world of business can quickly cover management theory and practice. Teaching goes beyond the classroom towards more hands-on experiential learning. Students can work towards careers in areas as diverse as analytics and digital marketing through their choice of optional modules.
summary: The Business Management BSc (Hons) 2025 is a 3-year full-time programme focusing on management expertise, adaptability, and emergent trends. It features unique optional modules from the second semester and is AACSB accredited.
entities: Association to Advance Collegiate Schools of Business (AACSB)

title: Business Management BSc (Hons) Curriculum Structure & Key Modules
text: What you will study As part of this programme, you can: explore current business and management challenges gain an understanding of key business management principles and practices, including accounting, marketing, finance, strategy and entrepreneurship tailor the programme to your interests by choosing optional modules learn directly from people in inspiring jobs through regular guest speakers from industry Our research-focused academics are leaders in their field and keep the programme current. You’ll benefit from our Business School’s close links with industry. Modules with a difference Innovation, Technology and the Environment is an inter-disciplinary module that is offered at two levels in parts 1 and 2. Open modules integrate the latest developments in management and business into the curriculum. Corporate Social Responsibility and Sustainable Business is a compulsory module that mirrors the growing importance of sustainability in modern society and contemporary business. Course modules Students take a number of core and compulsory modules before selecting optional modules based on their interest. The programmes are designed to ensure that students gain the fundamentals of their discipline and then add specialisation and perspective through available optional modules. Optional modules may include modules related to the core discipline as well as options built around contemporary, real-world trends that change from time to time. Find out why, when and how we might make changes. In Year 1, students will take 6 fixed course modules, and 2 optional electives. In Year 2, students will take 2 fixed course modules, and 6 optional electives. In Year 3, students will take 3 fixed course modules, and 4 optional electives.
summary: This programme covers core business management principles including accounting, marketing, finance, and entrepreneurship, featuring innovative modules like "Innovation, Technology and the Environment" and "Corporate Social Responsibility." Students tailor their studies with optional modules.
entities: Corporate Social Responsibility and Sustainable Business

title: Business Management BSc (Hons) Core and Optional Modules
text: In Year 1, students must take all these course modules:- Business in Society MANG1048 Digital Technologies in Business MANG1050 Introduction to Accounting and Finance MANG1055 Introduction to Management MANG1003 Introduction to Marketing for Business MANG1053 Management Analysis MANG1047 In Year 1, students must choose two of these optional electives:- Commercial Law MANG1014 Financial Accounting MANG1044 Introduction to Economics For Non-Economists ECON1009 Management Accounting MANG1045 In Year 2, students must take all these course modules:- Critical Perspectives on Organisation and Management MANG2088 Operations Management MANG2021 In Year 2, students must choose three of these optional electives:- Entrepreneurial Management MANG2094 Financial Accounting 2 MANG2003 Human Resource Management MANG2011 Management Ethics MANG2041 Company Law MANG2017 In Year 2, students must choose three of these optional electives:- Consulting: Context, Concepts and Practice MANG2104 Innovation, Technology and the Environment MANG2095 Making Successful Decisions MANG2069 Management Accounting 2 MANG2005 Philosophy of Management and Organisations MANG2057 In Year 3, students must take all these course modules:- Business Project MANG3100 Corporate Social Responsibility and Sustainable Business MANG3029 Strategic Management MANG3008 In Year 3, students must choose two of these optional electives:- Financial Accounting MANG3003 Information, Organisation and Accountability from a Historical Perspective MANG3065 Marketing in the Digital Age MANG3054 Technological Innovation MANG3072 In Year 3, students must choose two of these optional electives:- International Business MANG3105 Management Accounting 3 MANG3006 Management of Information Systems MANG3101 Project Management MANG3034
summary: This section details the specific core and optional modules for the Business Management BSc (Hons) programme across its three years, covering subjects from Business in Society and Digital Technologies to Strategic Management and International Business.

title: Business Management BSc (Hons) Career Paths & Employability Services
text: Your future career Graduating with a business management degree from a double-world top 100 university will open various career paths for you. You could choose to work in: the financial sector industry retail consultancy public sector Career opportunities Possible roles for Business Management graduates include: business advisor business analyst business development manager chartered management accountant corporate investment banker data analyst Companies you could work at University of Southampton graduates have secured roles in organisations such as: Deloitte Ernst & Young (EY) PricewaterhouseCoopers (PwC) Proctor & Gamble Times Group Careers services at University of Southampton Delhi As well as support from our team in Delhi, you’ll have access to our market-leading employability development offering. This includes: online resources regular workshops one-to-one guidance and support with qualified careers professionals through our MyCareer platform Through our e-mentoring scheme, you’ll also have access to: tailored career planning advice on employability skills industry insight We have incorporated our employability skills model, Successful Futures, into our curriculum. Our links with multi-national and industrial organisations mean that you can expect to engage with employers and explore potential career paths. You will receive a personalised employability programme that will provide excellent prospects for securing a premium placement and financial security on graduating. We also host regular alumni events hosted in person across major cities in India, allowing you to grow your own networks. Our support is ongoing, and once you graduate, you’ll be part of a global community of over 220,000 alumni that spans over 180 countries.
summary: Graduates of this Business Management programme pursue diverse careers in finance, industry, retail, consultancy, and public sectors, with roles like business advisor or data analyst. The University of Southampton Delhi offers extensive career support, including e-mentoring, workshops, and alumni networking.

title: Business Management BSc (Hons) Eligibility & English Language Criteria
text: Key information To help plan your year we have published University of Southampton Delhi term and holiday dates in advance, but these may be subject to change. We expect to provide 25 seats on this programme for academic year 25/26. Eligibility criteria Students from diverse backgrounds with a keen interest in business are encouraged to apply. They would require a basic level of mathematics. For Central Board of Secondary Education (CBSE): Minimum 78% in your India Senior School Certificate Examination (Standard XII) examination (overall) and a minimum of 75% in Mathematics in your Secondary School Examination (Standard X) examination For Council Indian Certificate of Secondary Education (CICSE): Minimum 78% in your Indian School Certificate Examination (Standard XII) examination (overall) and minimum of 75% in Mathematics in your Indian Certificate of Secondary Education (Standard X) examination For State Board: Minimum 83% in your Higher Secondary Certificate (Standard XII) examination (overall) and Minimum 75% in Mathematics in your Senior Secondary School Certificate (Standard X) examination International Baccalaureate: 34 points and above, including 17 points at Higher Level and 4 points in Maths and English at Standard level A levels: AAB, plus Grade B or above in GCSE Maths English language requirement We accept several ways of meeting our English language requirement. These include: IELTS Test, with scores of 6.5 overall (6.0 in reading, writing, speaking, and listening) other tests listed on our website an approved academic qualification having the required grade If you’ve been taught and assessed in English, including at a CBSE or CISCE school, for your final 4 years of high school, and have achieved at least 70% in English Language, we may be able to accept this as evidence of your English language ability. Refer to our English language proficiency guide for more information about English language requirements. Our academic admissions criteria Typical academic admissions criteria represent the usual level of academic attainment an applicant is expected to achieve prior to being admitted to that programme. These criteria are not intended to provide a guarantee that all applicants who satisfy the criteria may be offered a place, nor that all applicants to be made an offer will receive an identical offer to one another.
summary: The Business Management BSc (Hons) programme has 25 seats for the 25/26 academic year. Eligibility requires specific minimum percentages in Indian board exams (CBSE, CICSE, State Board) or scores in International Baccalaureate/A-levels, alongside an English language proficiency (e.g., IELTS 6.5 overall).
entities: Council Indian Certificate of Secondary Education (CICSE)

title: Business Management BSc (Hons) Tuition Fees & Scholarships
text: Our fees You can pay your tuition fee in up to 4 instalments. The fees for a year’s study, starting in August 2025, are: 13,20,000 INR for India students 15,40,000 INR for Overseas students Your first payment will be a deposit of 10% of the programme fee. To confirm your offer, you must pay your deposit within 28 days of receiving a conditional offer, and no later than 14 days before the start of term. For students starting in August 2025, the deposit is: 132,000 INR for India students 154,000 INR for Overseas students You’ll then pay the balance of your yearly tuition fee as you study, in up to 3 instalments. If you’re an India student, you’ll have to make 3 separate payments of: 316,800 INR by Monday 8 September 2025 435,600 INR by Monday 26 January 2026 435,600 INR by Monday 20 April 2026 If you’re an Overseas student, you’ll have to make 3 separate payments of: 369,600 INR by Monday 8 September 2025 508,200 INR by Monday 26 January 2026 508,200 INR by Monday 20 April 2026 We may adjust the fees in the future to account for inflation and changes in other costs. Exact payment dates for future years will vary according to the academic calendar. Funding Scholarships We offer competitive scholarships to academically gifted students and talented students who would benefit from study financially through a merit bursary. Funding support Please email us about the partnerships we have developed with trusted and reputable financial institutions keen to support University of Southampton Delhi students with a range of education loans.
summary: Tuition fees for the Business Management BSc (Hons) programme starting August 2025 are 13,20,000 INR for Indian students and 15,40,000 INR for Overseas students, payable in instalments after a 10% deposit. Scholarships and education loan support are available.

title: Business Management BSc (Hons) Application & Degree FAQs
text: Our application process Admissions are now open for a place at our campus in the Delhi NCR. Our first cohort for undergraduate bachelor’s degrees starts in August 2025. Potential students can understand more about our application process by looking at helpful guidance and advice on university admissions pages. Download more details This downloadable document contains an overview of the programme, its aims, the programme structure, information on course modules and shows how we assess student progress as they learn with us. PDF BSc Hons Business Management 2025 Programme Description Document 106 KB How to apply Our programmes are taught in English, and content is equivalent to that delivered to other University of Southampton students. To match the programme lengths of internationally respected UK awarded degrees, undergraduates study for three years, and postgraduates study for one year. You might also want to know Should I wait to make my application to study? We encourage students to make their application to study at University of Southampton Delhi as soon as they can. This means we can issue an early Conditional Offer and that students will have the best the chance to meet their offer conditions. If demand is high, we may choose to close applications earlier than mentioned, but students with Offers to study would receive preferential treatment. Can I get an offer with predicted scores? We encourage students to apply as soon as possible so that they can receive a Conditional Offer based on predicted scores for their chosen programme. We can accept an application from a student using their predicted grades if we receive their final grades once they are available. Why is an Honours degree good for me? An Honours degree, shown with (Hons) in the programme title, mean that students will understand the fundamentals of their discipline and then build specialisation and perspective through optional modules. These fast-paced, intense programmes deliver essential learning in the first academic year and offer optional course modules, or electives, in the second year. Honours degrees are offered at University of Southampton campuses across the world, reflecting teaching and learning to a higher academic standard, meaning that an Honours degree would hold more value post graduation for future study and career development. Why is my degree award BSc (Hons) or BEng (Hons)? Degrees with a BA and BSc award do not represent a stream, but a broad categorisation that reflects student choice and leaning. Our BSc (Hons) and BEng (Hons) undergraduate degrees are oriented towards specialist, technical roles in industry with an analytical approach, not science subjects. How can I earn an Honours degree in just 3 years? The University Grants Commission (UGC) has mandated University of Southampton Delhi to make sure that programmes delivered in India are the same as those delivered at University of Southampton in the UK. Three years is the standard length of an undergraduate Honours degree in England where University of Southampton sets academic standards. This 3 year length reflects a trend towards accelerated learning where students see a return on their tuition fees as quickly as possible, whilst still maintaining high academic standards and the quality of the degree awarded. University of Southampton 3 year undergraduate degrees, and 1 year postgraduate degrees, are accepted widely internationally. What is an option? Our programmes are designed to make sure that students quickly understand the fundamentals of their discipline, and then build specialisation and perspective through electives, or optional, modules. These optional modules are typically offered in academic years 2 and 3, adding depth and breadth to core learning from previous years. As these options are often built around real-world trends, the specific choice of module available to students from year to year may vary.
summary: Admissions for the August 2025 cohort are open at the Delhi NCR campus, with guidance available online and a downloadable programme document. FAQs address early applications, predicted scores, the value of an Honours degree, and the 3-year UK-equivalent undergraduate programme structure.
entities: BSc Hons Business Management 2025 Programme Description Document
